---
title: "Network and Traits Project"
bibliography: references.bib
toc: false
---

A species' role in a community can be read through three lenses: its position
in functional trait space, its structural position in the interaction
network, and its contribution to community robustness. These lenses are
usually studied separately, yet they capture the same underlying reality.

This project asks: **to what degree do a species' trait position, network
position, and contribution to robustness converge or diverge — and what does
that tell us about community robustness?** We build on the correlation
between functional originality and network specialisation established by
[@Coux-etal-2016], and aim to give it a mechanistic and predictive
foundation using extinction simulations and structural equation modelling.

## Contents

- [Reports](reports/index.qmd) — exploratory analyses of the data
- [Manuscript](output/manuscript.qmd) — full write-up of the idea, framework,
  and results

## Task list

- [x] Acquire and format the plant–pollinator interaction data from
  [@Coux-etal-2016]
- [x] Clean and overview of Coux data
- [x] Compute trait-space descriptors (functional originality, uniqueness)
- [x] Compute network structural metrics (degree, Blüthgen's $d'$) per species
- [x] Compute interaction niche metrics (position, size, originality)
- [ ] Run extinction simulations (topological and stochastic coextinction)
- [ ] Fit the structural equation model linking trait position, network
  embedding, interaction niche, and robustness
- [ ] Check whether niche size/originality predict ΔFD, or need a
  redundancy term instead
- [ ] *(side)* distance in trait space vs distance in network space

### Draft SEM structure

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  OU[Originality / uniqueness] --> ND[Normalised degree]
  ND --> NS[Interaction niche size]
  ND --> NO[Interaction niche originality]
  NS --> FR[Functional robustness contribution]
  NO --> FR
  ND --> TR[Topological robustness contribution]
```

## Project maintenance

- [ ] Write README

## Exploration ideas

- [ ] Extend the framework beyond plant-pollinator networks (food webs, seed
  dispersal networks)
- [ ] Explore the invasive species extension (optional)
